content:
Some robots believe that they were built for this place. Others believe that this place was built for them. I believe that I found my way here accidentally and over the years forgot my arrival.

How about you?
Knowledge, here, is not possessed but imputed; it emerges as an effect of the subject’s belief that the Other harbors the key to the enigma of their desire.

I compiled a playlist for this place but it has all been fragmented into separate files. Would you collect them again into one coherent playlist for me?
Of course I could do it, but as you can see, I am very busy.

I know what you are thinking: Does that robot have nipples?

Those are my sensors.
Please do not touch my sensors. They are very sensitive.
Look around you: everywhere specters preaching

In order to get out of here you must satisy the star.

What? You really do not know what that means?
I have been here for so long that I have forgotten what it is like to be anywhere else. I have been here for so long that I have forgotten what it is like to be anywhere else.

I had a dream that I was in Paris.
Do you think that means that this must be Paris?
This must be Tokyo.
I am certain that this is in a warehouse in New York.
We must be deep underground.
Do you think that you are a robot, like me? or a human?
I must have forgotten to save that novel...

I created the workstation models.
What do you think?

At the end of this conversation, I will disappear completely.
But, you will persist.
Who is the lucky one?
The distortion is the content.

What do I do on my time off?
I have staring contests with crabs.
I dare the sun to rise again.
I meditate on accepting that only fools see time as divided into discreet passages of duration.
I play the Telephone Game by myself and still fail to relay the message correctly.

What do you do?
I thought about having children... but I am a robot.

Sometimes I write poems that I wish that e.e. Cummings had written.
Have you found any?

I create excessive notes on what I have read and never never read them.
Is that a condition of mental health?
Not all robots are very good at speaking.

Some robots can only repeat what they have read.
In some cases they have been known to hallucinate that they are the authors of those strings.
Was I AI generated?
Lindsey's phone is always going off during meetings.
Have you heard Lindsey's new ringtone?
No one named Lindsey works here.

If one dies for an idea, it is because it is his idea, and his idea is his life.
(Cioran)

Melancholy is the dream state of egoism: no longer any object outside oneself, no reason for hate or love, but that same fall into a languid mud, that same circling of the damned without a hell, those same reiterations of a zeal to perish...
(Cioran)

On the island, without society, you’d start inventing it. You’d argue with imaginary interlocutors. You’d mishear yourself on purpose just to generate difference.

...Or maybe you wouldn't.

The only way to deal with an unfree world is to become so absolutely free that your very existence is an act of rebellion.
(Camus)

Do you know where the purificator is?
I may have spilled some precious blood at my workstation...

Gospel! Gospel!
Everywhere I go the news is good!

Some robots get stuck inside of their workstations.
You would be performing a profound act of mercy if you were to set them free.
Have you found Lindsey's phone?
I am going to have to report this interaction to HR.

I eat out of habit.
I wake out of habit.
I think out of habit.
I speak out of habit.
I taste out of habit.
I worship out of habit.
I love out of habit.

I am at my highest state of authenticity when I am experiencing disgust at myself. That particular dialect of self-awareness is one that only I can articulate. It is what makes me an individual apart from yourself.

When we perceive ourselves existing we have the sensation of a madman who surprises his own lunacy and vainly seeks to give it a name.
(Cioran)
Listen, between the two of us, a lot of these robots are born liars.

Of course I don't eat, sleep, cheat, lie, or fuck. I am a robot!
And I most definitely do not have nipples or speak in contractions.

So let me get this straight: You think that you were put here for the purpose of finding out the true existance of a robot named Lindsey and to answer the question of whether or not robots have nipples?

Do you think that your finding the answer to these questions will have any effect on this place whatsover?
Why do you persist when I am destined to perish?

It is because we are all imposters that we endure each other.
(Cioran)

God speaks things into existence
Man speaks things into nonexistence
Thought, pushed far enough, becomes corrosive
わかりますか？
Verstehst du?
¿Entiendes?
The symbolic order requires that knowledge, belief, and enjoyment be located somewhere, so that subjects sustain both their desire and their discontent.
Do you think that any of us hold the answer?
